qa_pairs.question: How can I request a single full A100-40gb GPU for my compute task on Narval?
qa_pairs.answer: To request a single full A100-40gb GPU, use the Slurm option `--gpus=a100:1`.

qa_pairs.question: What Slurm options are available for requesting multiple full A100-40gb GPUs per node on Narval?
qa_pairs.answer: To request multiple full A100-40gb GPUs per node, you can use Slurm options such as `--gpus-per-node=a100:2`, `--gpus-per-node=a100:3`, or `--gpus-per-node=a100:4`.

qa_pairs.question: How do I request multiple full A100-40gb GPUs to be scattered anywhere on Narval?
qa_pairs.answer: To request multiple A100-40gb GPUs scattered anywhere, use the Slurm option `--gpus=a100:n`, replacing `n` with the desired number.

qa_pairs.question: Does Narval support Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) technology?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, several GPU nodes on Narval are configured with Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) technology.

qa_pairs.question: What are the four available sizes of Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) instances on Narval?
qa_pairs.answer: The four available sizes for MIG instances are 1g.5gb, 2g.10gb, 3g.20gb, and 4g.20gb.

qa_pairs.question: What are the specifications of the 1g.5gb MIG instance?
qa_pairs.answer: The 1g.5gb MIG instance provides 1/8 of the compute power with 5 GB of GPU memory.

qa_pairs.question: What are the specifications of the 2g.10gb MIG instance?
qa_pairs.answer: The 2g.10gb MIG instance provides 2/8 of the compute power with 10 GB of GPU memory.

qa_pairs.question: What are the specifications of the 3g.20gb MIG instance?
qa_pairs.answer: The 3g.20gb MIG instance provides 3/8 of the compute power with 20 GB of GPU memory.

qa_pairs.question: What are the specifications of the 4g.20gb MIG instance, and how is its availability?
qa_pairs.answer: The 4g.20gb MIG instance provides 4/8 of the compute power with 20 GB of GPU memory. This version is available in smaller quantities.

qa_pairs.question: What Slurm option should I use to request a single 1g.5gb MIG instance?
qa_pairs.answer: To request a single 1g.5gb MIG instance for your compute task, use the Slurm option `--gpus=a100_1g.5gb:1`.

qa_pairs.question: What Slurm option should I use to request a single 2g.10gb MIG instance?
qa_pairs.answer: To request a single 2g.10gb MIG instance for your compute task, use the Slurm option `--gpus=a100_2g.10gb:1`.

qa_pairs.question: What Slurm option should I use to request a single 3g.20gb MIG instance?
qa_pairs.answer: To request a single 3g.20gb MIG instance for your compute task, use the Slurm option `--gpus=a100_3g.20gb:1`.

qa_pairs.question: What Slurm option should I use to request a single 4g.20gb MIG instance?
qa_pairs.answer: To request a single 4g.20gb MIG instance for your compute task, use the Slurm option `--gpus=a100_4g.20gb:1`.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find the recommended maximum quantities of CPU cores and system memory per GPU instance?
qa_pairs.answer: The recommended maximum quantities of CPU cores and system memory per GPU instance are listed in the table of characteristics for bundles.

qa_pairs.question: How can I track my compute tasks on Narval?
qa_pairs.answer: You can track your CPU and GPU compute tasks in real-time or view past tasks from the Narval portal at `https://portail.narval.calculquebec.ca/`.

qa_pairs.question: What are the benefits of tracking my tasks on the Narval portal?
qa_pairs.answer: Tracking your tasks helps to maximize the utilization of resources and decrease your wait times in the queue.

qa_pairs.question: What specific information can I visualize for a compute task on the Narval portal?
qa_pairs.answer: For a task, you can visualize the utilization of compute cores, memory used, and GPU utilization.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it important to adjust resource requests for compute tasks?
qa_pairs.answer: It is important to use the allocated resources and rectify your demands when compute resources are underutilized or not used at all.

qa_pairs.question: Can you provide an example of when I should adjust my resource request for a compute task?
qa_pairs.answer: For example, if you request four CPU cores but only use one, you should adjust your submission file accordingly.
